card.intuition: A set of features in React 18 that allows React to prepare multiple versions of your UI at the same time and switch between them without blocking the main thread.
card.analogy: A chef cooking multiple dishes at once (Multitasking): they can start the pasta, then while it's boiling, start the sauce. If a fire starts (Urgent User Input), they drop everything to fix it immediately, then go back to the food.
when_to_use: Responsive UIs during heavy data processing
Using `useTransition` to keep inputs fast while filtering large lists
gotchas: Concurrent rendering is NOT 'parallel' rendering (JS is still single-threaded); it's about 'interleavable' rendering
It requires 'Shared Mutability' to be avoided — your components must be pure functions

steps.label: Work Units
steps.annotation: React breaks render work into small fiber units that can be interrupted.

steps.label: Start Render
steps.annotation: React starts rendering — interruptible, runs during idle time.

steps.label: Interrupt
steps.annotation: Urgent update (user input) interrupts current render.

steps.label: Process Urgent
steps.annotation: Urgent work completes first — user sees immediate response.

steps.label: Resume
steps.annotation: React resumes the interrupted low-priority render from where it paused.

steps.label: Commit
steps.annotation: Commit phase: all DOM changes applied atomically — never half-rendered.

exercise.prompt: Identify the React 18 hook used to mark a state update as non-urgent.
